title: gaultheria pumila var. leucocarpa
intro: Gaultheria pumila var. leucocarpa, also known as the mountain cranberry, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of Japan and the Korean Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to its high concentration of organic acids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Gaultheria pumila var. leucocarpa include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Gaultheria pumila var. leucocarpa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused in hot water, or used as an ingredient in traditional Asian remedies, such as Kampo medicine.
